title: Battle of Cathar

{{Quote|Unspeakable cruelty. Pain. The death of a species, boiled away in the sea.|[[Ferroh]]|<ref name="KOTOR 42" />}}
The '''Battle of Cathar'''<ref name="KOTORCG">''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''</ref> was the second battle<ref name="Atlas" /> of the [[Mandalorian Wars]] before the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] invaded the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], as the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]] under the leadership of [[Mandalore the Ultimate]] conquered worlds just outside of the Republic space. It resulted in the virtual [[Speciecide|extinction]] of the [[Cathar/Legends|Cathar]] species. It would later be the event that resulted in [[Revan/Legends|Revan's]] [[Revanchist|Jedi faction]] joining the war.

==History==
===Prelude===
{{Quote|My people had a great reputation as warriors... and that appealed to the Mandalore version of 'honor'. They sought to test themselves against us, I think... test themselves by bombing our world, slaughtering my people while they slept or while they ran.|[[Juhani]]|<ref name="KOTOR" />}}
During the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]],<ref name="TSW1">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 1|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 1]]</ref> led by [[Mandalore the Indomitable]],<ref name="History of the Mandalorians">{{InsiderCite|80|The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors}}</ref> sided with [[Brotherhood of the Sith|the Sith]], led by [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] and [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]]. The [[Mandalorian Crusaders]] ravaged [[Foerost/Legends|Foerost]],<ref name="TSW1" /> [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]],<ref name="TSW2">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 2]]</ref> [[Iridonia/Legends|Iridonia]], the [[Abron system]], the [[Thisspias system/Legends|Thisspias system]], and the [[Contruum system/Legends|Contruum system]] under Qel-Droma's command.<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> The Cathar fought against the Mandalorians<ref name="Juhani">Established by in-game dialogue with [[Juhani]] in ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''.</ref> and the Mandalorians were [[Battle of Onderon (Great Sith War)|defeated]] on [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]]. Mandalore the Indomitable died on [[Dxun/Legends|Dxun]].<ref name="TSW6">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 6|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 6]]</ref>

[[Mandalore the Ultimate]], Mandalore the Indomitable's successor, rebuilt the Mandalorians by opening membership to many species. Under his leadership, the Mandalorians became the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]].<ref name="History of the Mandalorians" /> When the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|Sith]] suggested they invade the Republic, saying it would be a battle that would be remembered forever, the Mandalorians eagerly agreed.<ref name="KOTOR" /> 

To prepare for [[Mandalorian Wars|their invasion of the Republic]], the Mandalorians began conquering planets [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|outside]] Republic space.<ref name="KOTOR" /> The Mandalorians remembered the dishonor they had suffered at the hands of the Cathar, so Cathar became one of their earliest targets. [[Cassus Fett]], Mandalore's prime strategist, was assigned to lead the attack.<ref name="KOTOR 42" />

===Battle===
{{Quote|They swooped down from space across the world, firing at anything that moved. They used ships in space to destroy all orbital facilities and bombard the surface. We did resist, and in spite of their violent attack we did stave them off for quite a while. But in the end we were doomed…|Juhani|<ref name="KOTOR" />}}
The Mandalorians' attack caught the Cathar people completely off guard. Their military forces converged from space, bombarding the surface, first destroying all interstellar communications relays and orbital spacedock facilities, and then descending to the surface and slaughtering most of the planetary population while they slept or as they fled.<ref name="Juhani" /> Cassus Fett's [[Basilisk war droid/Legends|Basilisk war-mounts]] decimated the more primitive Cathar settlements.<ref name="KOTOR 42" />

Because they had fought against the Mandalorians in the Great Sith War, the Cathar realized why they'd been targeted, and that there would be no mercy for their species or their world. Cathar was not part of the Galactic Republic, so the Cathar were left to fend completely for themselves.<ref name="Juhani" /> Near the end of the battle, Cassus herded the surviving Cathar into the ocean and ordered that they be killed. [[Unidentified Mandalorian female|One Mandalorian]] objected, saying the Cathar had already been defeated. But Cassus said the battle was about revenge, and he intended to kill all Cathar on the planet. He then gave the order to have the remaining Cathar killed, and the Mandalorian who objected died with them.<ref name="KOTOR 42" />

The final act of the Cathar race was to pack the last able-bodied survivors who were able to withstand spaceflight into what few ships remained, sending them off into the void as quickly as possible. [[Juhani]]'s family escaped aboard one of these refugee flights, and landed on [[Taris/Legends|Taris]]. The Cathar species itself, though, was nearly rendered extinct as a consequence of the genocide of Cathar.<ref name="Juhani" />

==Aftermath==
{{Quote|Everyone's got some serious dirt in their history, ma'am. In the days of the Old Republic, we Mandalorians wiped out at least one sentient species just to prove that we could—the Cathar. Are we ashamed of that? I hope so.|[[Wad'e Tay'haai]], Mandalorian [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] and historian|<ref name="Imperial Commando: 501st">''[[Imperial Commando: 501st]]''</ref>}}
The Mandalorians didn't stop their conquest with Cathar. They continued to conquer systems outside Republic space and eventually invaded the Republic itself. The Mandalorian's smashed the Republic military in numerous battles and were on the verge of completely conquering the Republic. The [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], however, refused to enter the war because the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] thought there was an unseen threat that was more dangerous than the Mandalorians.<ref name="KOTOR" />

This changed in [[3963 BBY]]. The [[Revanchist]]s, a group of Jedi who wanted to defend the Republic in the war, came to Cathar to find proof of the Mandalorians' atrocities. The Jedi Council, which had recently [[Vindication|dealt with]] [[Jedi Covenant|another rogue Jedi faction]], sent a team of loyal Jedi, led by Council member [[Vrook Lamar]], to confront them and order them to disband. Soon after that, [[Revan/Legends|the Revanchists' leader]] found a [[Revan's mask|discarded Mandalorian mask]]. After this discovery, all the Jedi present then received a vision of the battle, proving the Mandalorians had virtually wiped out the Cathar. Outraged at what he had seen, the Revanchists' leader, who took the name ''Revan'', swore he wouldn't rest until the Mandalorians had been defeated. The knowledge of the battle proved to be the catalyst that convinced many Jedi to join Revan. The Council, meanwhile, sanctioned the Revanchists' actions, but still discouraged other Jedi from joining them.<ref name="KOTOR 42" />

==Behind the scenes==
Issue [[Knights of the Old Republic 42|forty-two]] of the [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'']], which was released in [[2009]] and set "approximately 3,963 years before the Battle of Yavin," first depicted the event via flashback. In that story, Ferroh stated in the present timeframe, "When I returned, a dozen years ago, my people had simply ''vanished''." The dating of the story and Ferroh's words place his return to Cathar in approximately 3975 BBY, and the Battle of Cathar in or before the year of his visit.<ref name="KOTOR 42" /> However, the earlier [[2008]] [[roleplaying game]] guidebook ''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' dated the event to 3973 BBY,<ref name="KOTORCG" /> as did the subsequent [[2012]] reference book ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''.<ref name="Warfare" /> The article has followed the most frequently used date.
